accepted.reason: Viterbi/forward-backward dynamic-programming kernels are high-value sequence-model optimization examples.
Lake pollution policy dynamics include repeated nonlinear updates and constrained control logic suitable for optimization.
Circle-circle and circle-line geometric collision checks are dense numeric kernels with clear compute structure.
Suffix-array compare/range binary search routines are algorithmically rich and transferable optimization targets.
Edge occurrence counting across shortest paths provides useful nested-loop graph-analysis logic.
Typed-memoryview summation is a compact low-level loop baseline for optimization behavior.

skipped.reason: Invalid parallelization over Python objects/iterators yields low-quality and potentially incorrect optimization signal.
Ray-tracing material classes depend on project-specific geometry types and object model, reducing standalone portability.
Point list generation/typed loop micro-example is too trivial and overlaps existing baseline kernels.
Cython compiler behavior tests are not representative algorithmic optimization workloads.
Database value-wrapper accessor is integration glue with minimal computational optimization value.
